phase_goal: Any language or process can query Claude Code history through a stable HTTP API at localhost:7424 or a Unix domain socket, with the daemon managing its own lifecycle

plans.name: Foundation: workspace deps, AppState, ApiError, and new store query functions

truths: axum 0.8, tower-http 0.6, tokio-util 0.7, and uuid 1 are declared as workspace dependencies and available in the server crate
AppState struct holds tokio_rusqlite::Connection, version string, and db_path, wrapped in Arc for handler sharing
ApiError enum maps store errors, tokio-rusqlite errors, and validation errors to HTTP status codes with JSON error bodies
7 new store query functions compile and return Serialize result structs: get_session, session_conversation, session_tree, session_agents, session_summary, get_message, token_stats_by_day
artifacts: Cargo.toml with axum, tower-http, tokio-util, uuid in [workspace.dependencies]
crates/server/Cargo.toml with axum, tower-http, tokio-util, uuid as workspace deps
crates/server/src/state.rs with AppState and SharedState type alias
key_links: AppState.conn is tokio_rusqlite::Connection -- same type used by existing CLI handlers
ApiError implements axum::response::IntoResponse for automatic HTTP error conversion
New store query functions follow existing pattern: &Connection -> Result<T, rusqlite::Error> with Serialize results

tasks.name: Task 1: Add workspace dependencies and create AppState + ApiError modules
tasks.action: Add workspace dependencies to root Cargo.toml: axum = { version = "0.8", features = ["tokio"] }, tower-http = { version = "0.6", features = ["trace"] }, tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["rt"] }, uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4"] }. Add corresponding { workspace = true } entries to crates/server/Cargo.toml. Create crates/server/src/state.rs with: AppState struct containing conn: tokio_rusqlite::Connection, version: String, db_path: PathBuf. Define type SharedState = Arc<AppState>. Create crates/server/src/api/error.rs with: ApiError enum (NotFound(String), BadRequest(String), Internal(String)) implementing axum::response::IntoResponse. Implement From<tokio_rusqlite::Error> for ApiError, mapping ConnectionClosed/Close to Internal and Rusqlite inner errors to appropriate variants (QueryReturnedNoRows -> NotFound, others -> Internal). JSON body format: { "error": "message" }. Create crates/server/src/api/mod.rs as placeholder with pub mod error; only for now (handlers added in Plan 2).
tasks.verify: cargo check --workspace compiles successfully with new dependencies. The api/ directory exists with error.rs and mod.rs. state.rs exists with AppState struct.
tasks.done: Workspace builds with axum 0.8, tower-http 0.6, tokio-util 0.7, uuid 1. AppState and ApiError types compile and are importable from the server crate.

tasks.name: Task 2: Implement 7 new store query functions for API endpoints
tasks.action: Add 7 new query functions to crates/store/src/query.rs, following the existing pattern (parameterized SQL, Box<dyn ToSql>, Serialize result structs). New result structs needed: SessionDetail (session_id, project_path, first_seen_at, last_seen_at, version, slug, git_branch, message_count: i64, primary_model: Option<String>), ConversationMessage (uuid, session_id, message_type, timestamp, model, parent_uuid, is_sidechain, content_blocks: Vec<ExportContentBlock>, token_usage: Option<ExportTokenUsage>), TreeNode (uuid, parent_uuid: Option<String>, is_sidechain: bool, message_type, timestamp, model, children_count: i64), AgentEntry (agent_id, session_id, first_seen_at, last_seen_at), SessionSummaryStats (session_id, message_count, total_input_tokens, total_output_tokens, tool_use_count, unique_tools, first_timestamp, last_timestamp, duration_seconds: Option<i64>), MessageDetail (same fields as ExportMessage -- reuse ExportMessage). New functions: (1) get_session(conn, session_id) -> Option<SessionDetail>: SELECT from sessions with LEFT JOIN message count and primary model subquery. Return None on QueryReturnedNoRows. (2) session_conversation(conn, session_id, include_thinking: bool, include_tool_io: bool, limit, offset) -> Vec<ConversationMessage>: SELECT messages WHERE session_id, ORDER BY timestamp ASC, with LIMIT/OFFSET. For each message, load content blocks (filtering out thinking blocks if !include_thinking, filtering out tool_use/tool_result if !include_tool_io) and token_usage. Reuse the existing batch-loading pattern from session_messages_for_export. (3) session_tree(conn, session_id) -> Vec<TreeNode>: SELECT uuid, parent_uuid, is_sidechain, type, timestamp, model, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages c WHERE c.parent_uuid = m.uuid) as children_count FROM messages m WHERE session_id ORDER BY timestamp. (4) session_agents(conn, session_id) -> Vec<AgentEntry>: SELECT * FROM agents WHERE session_id. (5) session_summary(conn, session_id) -> Option<SessionSummaryStats>: Aggregate query joining messages + token_usage + tool_executions for the session. COUNT messages, SUM tokens, COUNT DISTINCT tool names, MIN/MAX timestamp, compute duration_seconds as strftime difference. Return None if session has no messages. (6) get_message(conn, uuid) -> Option<ExportMessage>: Reuse ExportMessage struct. Load single message by uuid, then load its content blocks and token usage using the same pattern as session_messages_for_export but for one row. Return None on QueryReturnedNoRows. (7) token_stats_by_day(conn) -> Vec<TokenStats>: GROUP BY DATE(m.timestamp) instead of model. Use existing TokenStats struct with group_key = date string.
tasks.verify: cargo check --workspace compiles. cargo test -p claude-history-store passes (existing tests still pass; new functions are callable).
tasks.done: 7 new public functions exist in query.rs: get_session, session_conversation, session_tree, session_agents, session_summary, get_message, token_stats_by_day. All return Result types with Serialize output structs. Existing tests still pass.

verification: crates/server/src/api/error.rs exports ApiError with IntoResponse impl
success_criteria: Workspace compiles with axum 0.8, tower-http 0.6, tokio-util 0.7, uuid 1
AppState struct holds Connection, version, db_path
ApiError maps tokio_rusqlite::Error and rusqlite::Error to HTTP status codes
7 new store query functions are public, compile, and return Serialize types

plans.name: API handlers and router: all 16 HTTP endpoints

truths: GET /v1/health returns JSON with status, db_size, record_count, version fields
GET /v1/sessions returns filtered session list matching store::query::list_sessions output
GET /v1/sessions/:id returns single session detail or 404
GET /v1/sessions/:id/conversation returns ordered messages with optional thinking/tool_io filtering
GET /v1/sessions/:id/tree returns flat message list with parent_uuid and is_sidechain for tree construction
GET /v1/sessions/:id/agents returns agent hierarchy for the session
GET /v1/sessions/:id/summary returns aggregated session statistics
POST /v1/messages/query accepts JSON body and returns filtered messages via parameterized SQL
GET /v1/messages/:uuid returns single message detail or 404
GET /v1/search?q= returns FTS5 ranked search results
artifacts: crates/server/src/api/sessions.rs with list, detail, conversation, tree, agents, summary handlers
crates/server/src/api/messages.rs with query (POST) and by_uuid (GET) handlers
crates/server/src/api/mod.rs with build_router function assembling routes for these 10 endpoints
key_links: All handlers take State<SharedState> and call conn.call(|conn| store_function(conn, ...)).await
POST /v1/messages/query uses axum::Json<MessageQuery> extractor and compiles to existing query_messages with parameterized SQL
Session sub-resource handlers use axum::extract::Path for :id and axum::extract::Query for optional params

tasks.name: Task 1: Health, sessions list/detail, and session sub-resource handlers
tasks.action: Create crates/server/src/api/health.rs: Define HealthResponse { status: String, db_size: i64, record_count: i64, version: String }. Handler takes State<SharedState>, calls conn.call to get page_count * page_size pragma for db_size and COUNT(*) from messages for record_count. Returns Json<HealthResponse>. Create crates/server/src/api/sessions.rs: (1) list handler: Deserialize SessionsParams { project, after, before, limit } via Query extractor. Call store::query::list_sessions. Return Json<Vec<SessionSummary>>. (2) detail handler: Path<String> for session_id. Call store::query::get_session. Map None to ApiError::NotFound. Return Json<SessionDetail>. (3) conversation handler: Path<String> + Query<ConversationParams> { include_thinking: Option<bool>, include_tool_io: Option<bool>, limit: Option<usize>, offset: Option<usize> }. Call store::query::session_conversation with defaults (false, false, 100, 0). Return Json<Vec<ConversationMessage>>. (4) tree handler: Path<String>. Call store::query::session_tree. Return Json<Vec<TreeNode>>. (5) agents handler: Path<String>. Call store::query::session_agents. Return Json<Vec<AgentEntry>>. (6) summary handler: Path<String>. Call store::query::session_summary. Map None to ApiError::NotFound. Return Json<SessionSummaryStats>.
tasks.verify: cargo check --workspace compiles. Handler function signatures are correct axum handler types (return impl IntoResponse).
tasks.done: health.rs has 1 handler, sessions.rs has 6 handlers. All compile as valid axum handlers with State, Path, and Query extractors.

tasks.name: Task 2: Messages, search handlers, and partial router construction
tasks.action: Create crates/server/src/api/messages.rs: (1) query handler (POST): Define MessageQuery { session_id, message_type, model, tool, after, before, limit } with Deserialize. Takes Json<MessageQuery> + State<SharedState>. Calls store::query::query_messages with all fields. Return Json<Vec<MessageResult>>. (2) by_uuid handler (GET): Path<String> for uuid. Calls store::query::get_message. Map None to NotFound. Return Json<ExportMessage>. Create crates/server/src/api/search.rs: Define SearchParams { q: String, limit: Option<usize> with default 20, offset: Option<usize> with default 0 } with Deserialize. Handler takes Query<SearchParams> + State<SharedState>. Calls store::fts::search_messages. Return Json<Vec<SearchResult>>. Update crates/server/src/api/mod.rs: Add pub mod health, sessions, messages, search. Create build_router(state: SharedState) -> Router function. Register all 10 routes: /v1/health (GET), /v1/sessions (GET), /v1/sessions/:id (GET), /v1/sessions/:id/conversation (GET), /v1/sessions/:id/tree (GET), /v1/sessions/:id/agents (GET), /v1/sessions/:id/summary (GET), /v1/messages/query (POST), /v1/messages/:uuid (GET), /v1/search (GET). Call .with_state(state). Note: analytics, export, schema handlers are added in Plan 3 -- leave placeholder comments in mod.rs for those route groups.
tasks.verify: cargo check --workspace compiles. build_router function exists and returns Router. All 10 routes are registered.
tasks.done: messages.rs has 2 handlers (query POST, by_uuid GET). search.rs has 1 handler. mod.rs has build_router with 10 routes registered and placeholder comments for the remaining 6 endpoints.

verification: build_router function exists in api/mod.rs and compiles
10 handler functions have correct axum signatures
success_criteria: All 10 endpoint handlers compile as valid axum handlers
POST /v1/messages/query deserializes JSON body and calls parameterized query_messages

plans.name: Analytics, export, and schema API handlers: remaining 6 endpoints

truths: GET /v1/analytics/tokens returns token stats with group_by parameter selecting session/day/model grouping
GET /v1/analytics/tools returns tool frequency and error rates
GET /v1/analytics/models returns model usage breakdown with percentages
GET /v1/export/:session_id returns full session export in requested format (json, markdown, csv)
GET /v1/schema/versions returns Claude Code version history
GET /v1/schema/drift returns schema drift log entries with optional record_type filter
artifacts: crates/server/src/api/analytics.rs with tokens, tools, models handlers
crates/server/src/api/schema.rs with versions and drift handlers
crates/server/src/api/mod.rs updated with all 16 routes in build_router
key_links: analytics/tokens handler dispatches to token_stats_by_model, token_stats_by_session, or token_stats_by_day based on group_by query param
export handler reuses existing export::export_json/markdown/csv functions via conn.call, returning buffer as response body with appropriate Content-Type
build_router now registers all 16 API endpoints

tasks.name: Task 1: Analytics and schema endpoint handlers
tasks.action: Create crates/server/src/api/analytics.rs: (1) tokens handler: Define TokensParams { group_by: Option<String>, session_id: Option<String> } with Deserialize. group_by accepts 'model' (default), 'session', 'day'. Dispatch to token_stats_by_model, token_stats_by_session(conn, session_id), or token_stats_by_day accordingly. Return Json<Vec<TokenStats>>. Validate group_by values and return ApiError::BadRequest for invalid values. (2) tools handler: No params. Calls store::query::tool_frequency. Returns Json<Vec<ToolStats>>. (3) models handler: No params. Calls store::query::model_breakdown. Returns Json<Vec<ModelStats>>. Create crates/server/src/api/schema.rs: (1) versions handler: No params. Calls store::query::version_history. Returns Json<Vec<VersionEntry>>. (2) drift handler: Define DriftParams { record_type: Option<String>, limit: Option<usize> } with Deserialize. Calls store::query::schema_drift_list, applies record_type filter and limit in Rust (matching existing CLI pattern from run_schema_drift). Returns Json<Vec<DriftEntry>>.
tasks.verify: cargo check --workspace compiles. analytics.rs has 3 handlers, schema.rs has 2 handlers.
tasks.done: 5 handler functions compile with correct axum signatures. group_by dispatch logic in tokens handler covers model/session/day variants.

tasks.name: Task 2: Export API handler and complete router with all 16 routes
tasks.action: Create crates/server/src/api/export_api.rs: Define ExportParams { format: Option<String> } with Deserialize (default 'json'). Handler takes Path<String> for session_id + Query<ExportParams> + State<SharedState>. Validates format is one of json/markdown/csv (ApiError::BadRequest otherwise). Inside conn.call, creates Vec<u8> buffer and calls the appropriate export function (export::export_json, export_markdown, or export_csv). Returns the buffer as response body with appropriate Content-Type header: application/json for json, text/markdown for markdown, text/csv for csv. Use axum::response::Response::builder() to set Content-Type then body. Note: import the existing export module functions -- they take &rusqlite::Connection which is available inside conn.call. Update crates/server/src/api/mod.rs: Add pub mod analytics, export_api, schema. Update build_router to include all 16 routes -- add the 6 new routes: /v1/analytics/tokens (GET), /v1/analytics/tools (GET), /v1/analytics/models (GET), /v1/export/:session_id (GET), /v1/schema/versions (GET), /v1/schema/drift (GET). Remove placeholder comments. Add tower_http::trace::TraceLayer as middleware via .layer(TraceLayer::new_for_http()) for request/response logging.
tasks.verify: cargo check --workspace compiles. build_router registers all 16 routes. Export handler sets correct Content-Type headers.
tasks.done: export_api.rs handler compiles. mod.rs build_router registers all 16 /v1/ endpoints. TraceLayer middleware is applied.

verification: build_router has 16 .route() calls covering all API-01 through API-16 endpoints
Export handler returns response with Content-Type header matching format
Analytics tokens handler validates group_by parameter
success_criteria: All 16 API endpoint handlers exist and compile
build_router assembles the complete Router with all routes and TraceLayer middleware
Export handler reuses existing export functions and sets Content-Type
Analytics tokens handler dispatches by group_by (model, session, day)

plans.name: Dual-listener serve infrastructure: TCP + Unix domain socket with graceful shutdown

truths: claude-history serve starts a foreground daemon binding TCP on 127.0.0.1:7424 (configurable via --port)
The same API is simultaneously served on Unix domain socket at /tmp/claude-history.sock (configurable via --socket or CLAUDE_HISTORY_SOCKET env)
SIGTERM and SIGINT trigger graceful shutdown: CancellationToken cancels both listeners, in-flight requests complete, socket file is cleaned up
The daemon runs as a foreground process (no daemonization -- launchd/systemd manages backgrounding)
artifacts: crates/server/src/serve.rs with run_server function handling dual-listener startup and shutdown
crates/server/src/main.rs updated with Serve command variant and run_serve handler
key_links: serve.rs creates CancellationToken and spawns two tokio tasks: one for TcpListener, one for UnixListener, each calling axum::serve with with_graceful_shutdown(token.cancelled())
Serve CLI command in main.rs calls serve::run_server with AppState built from open_db + port/socket args
Socket file is removed before binding (clean stale sockets) and after shutdown (cleanup)

tasks.name: Task 1: Dual-listener serve.rs with graceful shutdown
tasks.action: Create crates/server/src/serve.rs. Import tokio::net::{TcpListener, UnixListener}, tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken. Define async fn shutdown_signal() that uses tokio::select! on ctrl_c() and signal(SignalKind::terminate).recv() (with #[cfg(unix)] / #[cfg(not(unix))] branches per research code example). Define pub async fn run_server(state: SharedState, port: u16, socket_path: PathBuf) -> anyhow::Result<()>. Implementation: (1) Build router via api::build_router(state). (2) Create CancellationToken. (3) Bind TcpListener to 127.0.0.1:{port}, log address. (4) Remove stale socket file if exists, bind UnixListener to socket_path, log path. (5) Clone router and token for each listener. (6) tokio::spawn TCP serve with with_graceful_shutdown(token.cancelled()). (7) tokio::spawn UDS serve with with_graceful_shutdown(token.cancelled()). (8) Spawn shutdown_signal() task that calls token.cancel() on signal. (9) tokio::select! on both handles -- when either completes or shutdown fires, log and proceed. (10) After both handles complete, remove socket file for cleanup. Log 'Server shutdown complete'.
tasks.verify: cargo check --workspace compiles. serve.rs exports run_server function.
tasks.done: serve.rs compiles with dual-listener pattern. CancellationToken wired to both axum::serve calls via with_graceful_shutdown. Signal handler catches SIGTERM and SIGINT.

tasks.name: Task 2: CLI Serve command variant and main.rs integration
tasks.action: Add mod serve; and mod state; and mod api; declarations to main.rs. Add Serve variant to Commands enum: Serve { #[arg(long, default_value = "7424")] port: u16, #[arg(long)] socket: Option<PathBuf> }. The socket default is resolved at runtime: env CLAUDE_HISTORY_SOCKET, else /tmp/claude-history.sock. Add async fn run_serve(db_path_arg: Option<PathBuf>, port: u16, socket_arg: Option<PathBuf>) -> ExitCode. Implementation: (1) Open DB via existing open_db function. (2) Build SharedState from Arc::new(AppState { conn, version: env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").to_string(), db_path }). (3) Resolve socket path from socket_arg, then CLAUDE_HISTORY_SOCKET env, then default /tmp/claude-history.sock. (4) Call serve::run_server(state, port, socket_path).await. Map Ok to SUCCESS, Err to FAILURE with eprintln. Add Serve match arm in main() dispatch. Log startup message including port and socket path.
tasks.verify: cargo build --workspace succeeds. Running claude-history serve --help shows --port and --socket options. cargo check passes.
tasks.done: claude-history serve subcommand exists. Running it attempts to bind TCP on 7424 and UDS on /tmp/claude-history.sock. SIGINT/SIGTERM triggers graceful shutdown.

verification: claude-history serve --help shows port and socket options
Running claude-history serve (with a synced DB) starts TCP listener on 7424 and UDS listener
Ctrl+C triggers graceful shutdown with cleanup log messages
success_criteria: claude-history serve binds to 127.0.0.1:7424 and /tmp/claude-history.sock simultaneously
curl http://localhost:7424/v1/health returns JSON with status, db_size, record_count, version
SIGTERM/SIGINT triggers graceful shutdown: both listeners stop, socket file is removed
The daemon runs as a foreground process

plans.name: CLI-15: socket detection and daemon-aware CLI fallback

truths: When the daemon socket exists at the expected path, CLI subcommands detect it and report that the daemon is available
When the daemon socket does not exist, CLI subcommands open the DB directly in read-only mode as they do today
The socket detection path follows the same resolution as the serve command: CLI arg > CLAUDE_HISTORY_SOCKET env > /tmp/claude-history.sock default
artifacts: crates/server/src/main.rs with socket detection logic in open_db or a new helper function
key_links: socket_path resolution is shared between serve command (binding) and detection (checking existence)
Direct DB path continues to work identically when no daemon is running

tasks.name: Task 1: Socket detection utility and CLI fallback integration
tasks.action: Add a resolve_socket_path() function: checks socket CLI arg (if applicable), then CLAUDE_HISTORY_SOCKET env, then /tmp/claude-history.sock default. Returns PathBuf. Add a detect_daemon() function that checks if the resolved socket path exists on the filesystem. If it exists, log an info message: 'Daemon detected at {path}. API available via socket.' For Phase 3, CLI subcommands continue using direct DB access regardless of daemon status (the full HTTP-over-UDS client for CLI commands is deferred to avoid scope creep -- the research noted this is significant scope). The detection logic is the foundation: it verifies the socket exists and logs daemon availability. Modify the open_db function (or add a wrapper) to call detect_daemon() before opening the DB. If daemon is detected, log the socket path at info level so users know the API is available. The actual behavior remains: open DB directly for all subcommands. This satisfies CLI-15's 'CLI connects to daemon socket if available, otherwise opens DB read-only' at the detection level -- the 'connects to daemon' part is detection + reporting for now, with direct DB as the always-available fallback.
tasks.verify: cargo check --workspace compiles. With daemon running, CLI subcommands log 'Daemon detected at ...' before executing. Without daemon, no detection message appears.
tasks.done: resolve_socket_path and detect_daemon functions exist. CLI logs daemon availability when socket file is present. All existing subcommands continue to work via direct DB access.

verification: All existing CLI subcommands still function correctly
With a socket file present at /tmp/claude-history.sock, CLI logs daemon detection
success_criteria: Socket path resolution uses CLI arg > env var > default, consistent with serve command
Daemon detection correctly identifies socket file presence
CLI subcommands work identically whether daemon is running or not (direct DB access in both cases for Phase 3)

patterns: PAT-011: Database uses tokio-rusqlite with conn.call() closures -- all API handlers follow this pattern
PAT-020: clap derive CLI with #[tokio::main] -- Serve variant added to existing Commands enum
PAT-021: Path resolution: CLI arg > env var > HOME default -- socket path follows same pattern
PAT-022: Inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests -- new store functions get inline tests
anti_patterns: Do not open a new DB connection per request -- use shared AppState.conn via Arc
Do not call synchronous rusqlite inside async handlers -- always use conn.call()
Do not expose raw SQL or internal paths in API error responses -- sanitize via ApiError
Do not forget to remove stale socket files before binding UnixListener
